title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: importer returns typed object:
code: gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta") pca <- import_pca(   ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenvec"),   type = "gcta",   eigenval = ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenval"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) admix <- import_admix(   ggpop_extdata("admixture"),   type = "admixture",   ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) stats <- import_stats(   ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy"),   type = "pixy" ) class(gwas) #> [1] "ggpop_gwas" "data.frame" class(pca) #> [1] "ggpop_pca"  "data.frame" class(admix) #> [1] "ggpop_admix" "data.frame" class(stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame"

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: Every module two user-facing plotting paths. Use direct plot_*() function want reference plot immediately, use ggpop() plus module geom_*() want compose ggplot layers. direct path:  ggplot extension path:  pattern applies across modules:
code: gwas |>   plot_manha(title = "GCTA Manhattan") gwas |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() gwas |> ggpop() + ggpop::geom_qq() pca |> ggpop() + geom_pca() admix |> ggpop() + geom_admix(k = 3, order_group = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: Population grouping uses simple two-column file: file drives PCA colours admixture group labels:   categorical colours use unified discrete palette entry:
code: head(import_pop_group(ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt"))) #>   sample_id  pop #> 1      P001 PopC #> 2      P004 PopB #> 3      P006 PopC #> 4      P009 PopA #> 5      P010 PopB #> 6      P012 PopB pca |> plot_pca(title = "GCTA PCA by population") admix |> plot_admix(k = 3, order_group = TRUE, show_group_labels = TRUE) ggpop_palette(4, "population") #> [1] "#4E79A7" "#59A14F" "#76B7B2" "#EDC948" ggpop_palette(8, "admixture") #> [1] "#4E79A7" "#F28E2B" "#E15759" "#76B7B2" "#EDC948" "#9C755F" #> [7] "#2F4B7C" "#FF7C43"

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: statistics module uses tidy pattern windowed summaries pixy vcftools outputs:  layered path filters facets requested statistics:
code: stats |>   plot_stats(stat = "all", chr = "chr2L") stats |>   ggpop() +   geom_stats(stat = c("fst", "pi"), chr = "chr2L")

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: Use plot_manha() ggpop() + geom_manha() Manhattan plots. Use plot_qq() ggpop() + ggpop::geom_qq() Q-Q plots. Use plot_pca() ggpop() + geom_pca() PCA plots. Use plot_admix() ggpop() + geom_admix() admixture plots. Use plot_stats() ggpop() + geom_stats() windowed population statistics. Treat direct plot_*() functions reference style; geom_*() look inside ggplot composition. Use compatibility article need original pophelper workflows.

title: Admixture plots
code: admix <- import_admix(   ggpop_extdata("admixture"),   type = "admixture",   ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) plot_admix(admix, k = "all") plot_admix(admix, k = c(2, 4)) ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3)

title: Admixture plots
text: pop_group.txt uses two columns, sample pop. joined import: native ggplot implementation mirrors supported pophelper plotQ() behavior : individual labels show_sample_labels = TRUE; sorting individuals sort = "", sort = "label", cluster name K1; group labels show_group_labels = TRUE; sorting group labels using order_group = TRUE.  layered ggplot path returns kind ggplot object:
code: head(import_pop_group(ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt"))) #>   sample_id  pop #> 1      P001 PopC #> 2      P004 PopB #> 3      P006 PopC #> 4      P009 PopA #> 5      P010 PopB #> 6      P012 PopB plot_admix(   admix,   k = 3,   sort = "all",   order_group = TRUE,   show_group_labels = TRUE,   show_sample_labels = TRUE ) ggpop(admix) +   geom_admix(k = 3, sort = "all", order_group = TRUE)

title: Admixture plots
text: plot_admix() direct plot wrapper. geom_admix() layered interface want build larger ggplot object . routes use visual defaults; plot_admix() reference style, ggpop(admix) + geom_admix() reproduces publication-level look inside ggplot composition.
code: plot_admix(admix, k = 3) ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3)

title: Admixture plots
text: import_admixture() also accepts limited STRUCTURE-style inputs. importer always returns typed long-format object.
code: structure <- import_admixture("structure_results.out", type = "structure") plot_admix(structure, k = "all")

title: Discrete color schemes
code: df <- data.frame(   pop = rep(c("PopA", "PopB", "PopC", "PopD"), each = 5),   pc1 = rnorm(20),   pc2 = rnorm(20) )  ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(pc1, pc2, colour = pop)) +   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_colour_ggpop("population") +   ggplot2::theme_classic()

title: Discrete color schemes
code: my_pop_colors <- new_pop_palette(   c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B", "#59A14F", "#E15759"),   name = "my population palette" )  ggpop_palette(6, my_pop_colors) #> [1] "#4E79A7" "#B0855C" "#D39132" "#779D47" "#8F8353" "#E15759"

title: Compatibility and advanced helpers
text: helpers keep original pophelper return structure.
code: plot_pophelper_q(import_admix("runs/", type = "admixture"), returnplot = TRUE) plot_admixture_pophelper(import_admix("runs/", type = "admixture"))

title: Compatibility and advanced helpers
text: kept compatibility older prompt-generated examples. Use import_gwas() plot_manha() new code.
code: improt_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") prot_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta")

title: Compatibility and advanced helpers
text: as_ggpop = TRUE, compatibility input converted typed ggpop_admix object can enter plotting pipeline native imports.
code: read_pophelper_q(files = "run1.Q", as_ggpop = TRUE)

title: Full extdata gallery
code: gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta") plot_manha(gwas, title = "GCTA Manhattan") plot_qq(gwas, title = "GCTA Q-Q")

title: Full extdata gallery
code: pca <- import_pca(   ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenvec"),   type = "gcta",   eigenval = ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenval"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) plot_pca(pca, title = "GCTA PCA")

title: Full extdata gallery
text: data also works layered path:
code: admix <- import_admix(   ggpop_extdata("admixture"),   type = "admixture",   ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) plot_admix(admix, k = "all", title = "ADMIXTURE", order_group = TRUE) ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3, order_group = TRUE)

title: GWAS plots
code: gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta") plot_manha(gwas, title = "GCTA Manhattan") plot_qq(gwas, title = "GCTA Q-Q")

title: GWAS plots
text: plot_manha(gwas) ggpop(gwas) + geom_manha() share default threshold suggestive reference lines.
code: gwas |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() gwas |>   ggpop() +   ggpop::geom_qq()

title: GWAS plots
text: GWAS module uses unified discrete palette entry rest ggpop. Manhattan plots default one discrete colour per chromosome:  two-colour alternating Manhattan plot, set binary = TRUE pass either ggpop palette name two explicit colours:  Reference-line colours use publication palette default, can overridden explicitly threshold_color suggestive_color.
code: plot_manha(gwas, palette = "manhattan") plot_manha(   gwas,   palette = c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B"),   binary = TRUE )

title: GWAS plots
text: import_gwas() accepts type = "gcta", type = "gemma", type = "emmax". package also keeps typo-compatible aliases old prompts notebooks. compatibility helpers, recommended new code: GWAS plotting uses ggpop’s internal ggplot implementation direct layered workflows.
code: improt_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") prot_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta")

title: PCA plots
text: imported object pop column, plotting routes map population groups unified ggpop discrete colour scale.
code: pca <- import_pca(   ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenvec"),   type = "gcta",   eigenval = ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenval"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) plot_pca(pca, title = "GCTA PCA") ggpop(pca) + geom_pca(pc_x = 1, pc_y = 3) ggpop(pca) +   geom_pca(pc_x = 1, pc_y = 2, palette = "population")

title: PCA plots
text: axis labels can carry variance explained eigenvalues available.
code: plot_pca(pca, pc_x = 1, pc_y = 2)

title: PCA plots
text: compute_pca(method = "flashpca") available flashpcaR installed. intentionally forced vignette build depends genotype object.
code: compute_pca(genotype, method = "flashpca")

title: Population genomics statistics
text: bundled pixy example dense chr2L windowed data set. contains 4,000 tidy rows across five statistics: 500 pairwise Dxy windows, 500 pairwise FST windows, 1,000 within-population windows pi, Tajima’s D, Watterson’s theta. Windows 100 bp wide span positions 1–50,000. can also import selected files explicitly:
code: pixy_dir <- ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy") stats <- import_stats(pixy_dir, type = "pixy") class(stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame" unique(stats$stat) #> [1] "dxy"             "fst"             "pi"              #> [4] "tajima_d"        "watterson_theta" range(stats$start) #> [1]     1 49901 selected <- import_stats(   pixy_dir,   pi = "pixy_pi.txt",   fst = "pixy_fst.txt",   tajima = "pixy_tajima_d.txt",   type = "pixy" ) unique(selected$stat) #> [1] "dxy"             "fst"             "pi"              #> [4] "tajima_d"        "watterson_theta"

title: Population genomics statistics
text: vcftools results use different column naming convention, enter typed plotting object. Supported examples include .windowed.pi, .windowed.weir.fst, .Tajima.D files. Explicit file selection works way:
code: vcftools_dir <- ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "vcftools") vcftools_stats <- import_stats(vcftools_dir, type = "vcftools") class(vcftools_stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame" unique(vcftools_stats$stat) #> [1] "tajima_d" "pi"       "fst" vcftools_selected <- import_stats(   vcftools_dir,   pi = "vcftools.windowed.pi",   fst = "vcftools.windowed.weir.fst",   tajima = "vcftools.Tajima.D",   type = "vcftools" ) unique(vcftools_selected$stat) #> [1] "tajima_d" "pi"       "fst"

title: Population genomics statistics
text: default, stat = "" stacks selected statistics vertically keeps x-axis aligned. mirrors usual pixy plotting layout comparing windowed summaries across genomic coordinate system. bundled example, panels use chr2L 100 bp windows across first 50 kb.  plotting path works vcftools imports:
code: plot_stats(stats, stat = "all", chr = "chr2L") plot_stats(vcftools_stats, stat = "all", chr = "chr2L")

title: Population genomics statistics
text: Use stat choose one summaries, chr select chromosomes, start / end focus windows overlapping region.   Multiple chromosomes can selected. one chromosome shown, default plot uses points avoid implying continuous genomic distance chromosomes. bundled pixy example contains chr2L, multi-chromosome example uses vcftools import multiple chromosomes present user data.
code: plot_stats(stats, stat = c("fst", "pi"), chr = "chr2L") plot_stats(stats, chr = "chr2L", start = 1, end = 20000) plot_stats(stats, chr = c("chr1", "chr2"))

title: Population genomics statistics
text: layered API follows grammar modules:  module uses ggpop’s shared theme, font sizing, discrete palette interfaces. Use palette, base_size, base_family way GWAS PCA modules.
code: stats |>   ggpop() +   geom_stats(stat = c("fst", "pi"), chr = "chr2L")

title: Authors and Citation
text: ggpop Authors. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Authors g (2026). ggpop: ggplot2 Extensions Population Genetics Plots. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://wwz33.github.io/ggpop/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggpop: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots},   author = {ggpop Authors},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://wwz33.github.io/ggpop/}, }

what: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: goal ggpop streamline publication-ready population-genomics visualization R. combines typed import helpers, direct plotting functions, composable ggplot2 extension layers GWAS, PCA, admixture results. also includes population genomics statistics module windowed FST, pi, Tajima’s D, Dxy, Watterson’s theta summaries. ggpop focuses tidy workflow: paths return ggplot objects. direct plot_*() functions define publication-style visual contract, matching geom_*() layers follow defaults inside ggpop() pipeline. GWAS Manhattan plots support explicit palette control:
code: import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   plot_manha()  import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() plot_manha(gwas, palette = "publication") plot_manha(gwas, palette = c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B"), binary = TRUE) plot_manha(gwas, threshold_color = "#E15759", suggestive_color = "#4E79A7")

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: can install development version GitHub : core package uses CRAN-available dependencies native plotting. GWAS module includes internal fastman-style Manhattan Q-Q plotting logic, ordinary GWAS plots require installing fastman. flashpcaR compute_pca(method = "flashpca"); pophelper direct plotQ() compatibility helpers. Dependency repository policy: pophelper unmodified points original upstream repository. flashpcaR required Windows source-install fixes points https://github.com/WWz33/flashpca.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("WWz33/ggpop")

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: main workflows. Also look getting started guide full documentation.   pop_group optional plot time:  pop_group optional plot time: Population groups use simple two-column sample pop file:
code: library(ggpop)  import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   plot_manha(title = "GCTA Manhattan") import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() import_pca(   "plink.eigenvec",   type = "plink",   pop_group = "pop_group.txt" ) |>   plot_pca(title = "PCA by population") plot_pca(pca, pop_group = FALSE) import_admix(   "admixture_results/",   type = "admixture",   ind = "samples.fam",   pop_group = "pop_group.txt" ) |>   plot_admix(k = 3, sort = "all", order_group = TRUE) plot_admix(admix, k = 3, pop_group = FALSE) import_admix(   "admixture_results/",   type = "admixture",   ind = "samples.fam",   pop_group = "pop_group.txt" ) |>   ggpop() +   geom_admix(k = 3, sort = "all", order_group = TRUE) sample  pop P001    PopA P002    PopB import_stats("pixy_results/", type = "pixy") |>   plot_stats(stat = "all", chr = "chr2L") import_stats("vcftools_results/", type = "vcftools") |>   plot_stats(stat = "all", chr = "chr2L")

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: recommended user-facing API intentionally small. Advanced compatibility helpers remain available users need direct backend behavior, ordinary workflows prefer import_*() |> plot_*() import_*() |> ggpop() + geom_*() interfaces.

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: ggpop provides unified discrete palette entry population-genomics categorical variables. PCA population colours admixture cluster fills use publication-oriented palette system default.
code: ggpop_palette(5, "population") ggpop_palette(5, "admixture") scale_colour_ggpop("population") scale_fill_ggpop("admixture")

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: version includes dependency fixes needed reliable source installation: replaced flashpcaR/flashpcaR/src/*.cpp src/*.h path stubs real source files; changed flashpcaR/flashpcaR/src/Makevars Makevars.win CXX11 CXX14; embedded fastman-style Manhattan Q-Q plotting behavior native ggplot layers.

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: GWAS guide Manhattan Q-Q plotting workflows PCA guide PCA imports, population colours, plotting Admixture guide ADMIXTURE/STRUCTURE imports, group labels, sorting Population statistics guide Windowed FST, pi, Tajima’s D, Dxy, Watterson’s theta plotting

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: ggpop builds ggplot2 follows tidy plotting conventions inspired packages tidyplots. Optional compatibility paths reference flashpcaR pophelper, GWAS Manhattan Q-Q plots use native ggplot layers fastman-style data transformation layout.

title: Adjust ggpop font settings — adjust_font
text: Apply consistent text scale ggpop/ggplot object.

title: Adjust ggpop font settings — adjust_font
code: adjust_font(   plot,   base_size = 11,   base_family = "",   title_size = NULL,   subtitle_size = NULL,   caption_size = NULL,   axis_title_size = NULL,   axis_text_size = NULL,   legend_title_size = NULL,   legend_text_size = NULL,   strip_text_size = NULL,   face = NULL )

title: Adjust ggpop font settings — adjust_font
text: plot ggplot object. base_size Base font size points. base_family Base font family. title_size, subtitle_size, caption_size Optional sizes plot title, subtitle, caption. axis_title_size, axis_text_size Optional sizes axis titles text. legend_title_size, legend_text_size Optional sizes legend title text. strip_text_size Optional size facet strip text. face Optional font face passed text elements.

title: Adjust ggpop font settings — adjust_font
text: ggplot object.

title: Admixture barplot layer — geom_admix
text: Adds stacked admixture proportion barplot layer `ggpop_admix` data. recommended ggplot extension path `import_admix(...) |> ggpop() + geom_admix(k = ...)`, visual style powers `plot_admix()`. native implementation mirrors supported pophelper `plotQ()` behavior individual labels, sample sorting, group labels, sorting group labels returning ggplot object.

title: Admixture barplot layer — geom_admix
code: geom_admix(mapping = NULL, data = NULL, ...,   sort = c("none", "cluster", "all", "label"), sortind = NULL,   k = "all", palette = NULL, group = "pop", order_group = FALSE,   show_group_labels = NULL, subset_group = NULL, bar_width = 1,   show.legend = FALSE, show_sample_labels = FALSE,   indlabwithgrplab = FALSE, indlabsep = " ", indlabsize = 5,   indlabangle = 90, indlabvjust = 0.5, indlabhjust = 1,   indlabcol = "grey30", indlabspacer = 0, grplabsize = 7,   grplabcol = "grey30", grplabbgcol = "#DCDCDC",   show_y_axis = FALSE, show_ticks = FALSE, ticksize = 0.1,   ticklength = 0.03, base_size = 5, base_family = "",   legend_position = "top", na.rm = FALSE, inherit.aes = TRUE)

title: Admixture barplot layer — geom_admix
text: mapping Ignored. Admixture layers require pophelper-style stacked Q bars. data Optional layer data. ... Additional layer parameters. sort, sortind Sample ordering strategy: `none`, ``, `cluster`, `label`, cluster name `K1`. `sortind` pophelper-compatible alias. k K selection: `""`, single integer, integer vector. palette Optional cluster palette publication style. group Population group column, usually `pop` `import_pop_group()`. order_group, show_group_labels, subset_group Pophelper-style group ordering, strip display, subsetting controls. bar_width Admixture bar width. indlabwithgrplab, indlabsep, indlabsize, indlabangle, indlabvjust, indlabhjust, indlabcol, indlabspacer Individual label controls aligned pophelper naming. grplabsize, grplabcol, grplabbgcol Group facet strip controls aligned pophelper naming. show_y_axis, show_ticks, ticksize, ticklength Axis tick controls aligned pophelper defaults. base_size, base_family, legend_position Admixture plot theme settings. na.rm Remove missing values. show.legend Legend display. show_sample_labels Display sample labels x axis. inherit.aes Inherit plot aesthetics.

title: Manhattan plot layer — geom_manha
text: Adds Manhattan plot list ggplot layers: data layer, threshold lines, fastman-style scales, default module theme. `geom_manha()` ggplot extension path paired direct `plot_manha()`.

title: Manhattan plot layer — geom_manha
code: geom_manha(mapping = ggplot2::aes(chr = .data$chr, pos = .data$pos, p = .data$p),   data = NULL, geom = "point", position = "identity", ..., threshold = 5e-8,   suggestive = 1e-5, threshold_color = .gwas_threshold_color(),   suggestive_color = .gwas_suggestive_color(),   size = 0.9, shape = 20, speedup = TRUE, logp = TRUE, maxP = 14,   bybp = FALSE, palette = "manhattan", binary = FALSE, base_size = 11,   base_family = "", na.rm = FALSE, show.legend = FALSE, inherit.aes = TRUE)

title: Manhattan plot layer — geom_manha
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Defaults `chr`, `pos`, `p`. data Optional layer data. geom Geom used rendering. position Position adjustment. ... Additional layer parameters. threshold Genome-wide p-value threshold line. suggestive Suggestive p-value threshold line. size, shape, speedup Point appearance fastman-style duplicate compression. logp Transform p-values `-log10(p)`, following ggpop's internal fastman-style layout. maxP Maximum displayed transformed value. bybp Display position Mb rather combined chromosome scale. palette ggpop palette name character vector hex colours. binary `TRUE`, repeat two colours across chromosomes binary alternating Manhattan palette. `FALSE`, assign one discrete colour per chromosome. base_size Base font size default module theme. base_family Base font family default module theme. threshold_color, suggestive_color Reference-line colours. Defaults use unified ggpop publication palette can overridden explicitly. na.rm Remove missing values. show.legend Legend display. inherit.aes Inherit plot aesthetics.

title: PCA scatter layer — geom_pca
text: Adds PCA plot list ggplot layers: point layer, PC axis labels variance contribution available, unified discrete population colour scale `pop` present, tidyplot-style ggpop theme `theme_tidyplot()`. `geom_pca()` ggplot extension path paired direct `plot_pca()`.

title: PCA scatter layer — geom_pca
code: geom_pca(mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data$pc1, y = .data$pc2),   data = NULL, ..., pc_x = 1, pc_y = 2, base_size = 11,   base_family = "", palette = NULL, pop_group = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA, inherit.aes = TRUE)

title: PCA scatter layer — geom_pca
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Defaults PC1 PC2. data Optional layer data. ... Additional parameters passed `geom_point()`. pc_x Principal component index x. pc_y Principal component index y. base_size Base font size tidyplot-style ggpop theme. base_family Base font family tidyplot-style ggpop theme. palette Discrete ggpop palette name hex vector population colours. pop_group Use imported `pop` metadata population colours available. na.rm Remove missing values. show.legend Legend display. inherit.aes Inherit plot aesthetics.

what: Q-Q plot layer for GWAS p-values — geom_qq
title: Q-Q plot layer for GWAS p-values — geom_qq
text: Adds Q-Q plot list ggplot layers using core p-value cleaning, truncation, speedup, red diagonal, lambda annotation contract ggpop's internal fastqq-style layout, applying tidyplot-style ggpop theme `theme_tidyplot()`. `geom_qq()` ggplot extension path paired direct `plot_qq()`.

title: Q-Q plot layer for GWAS p-values — geom_qq
code: geom_qq(mapping = ggplot2::aes(p = .data$p), data = NULL, geom = "point",   position = "identity", ..., size = 0.8, alpha = 0.8, colour = "black",   diagonal = TRUE, diagonal_colour = "red", show_lambda = TRUE,   maxP = 14, fix_zero = TRUE, speedup = TRUE, base_size = 11,   base_family = "", lambda_size = base_size * 0.9, na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = FALSE, inherit.aes = TRUE)

title: Q-Q plot layer for GWAS p-values — geom_qq
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Defaults `p`. data Optional layer data. geom Geom used rendering. position Position adjustment. ... Additional layer parameters. na.rm Remove missing values. size, alpha, colour Point appearance publication style. diagonal Draw expected-equals-observed diagonal. diagonal_colour Diagonal line colour. show_lambda Display genomic inflation factor. maxP Maximum displayed `-log10(p)` value, following ggpop's internal fastqq-style layout. fix_zero Replace zero p-values minimum non-zero p-value log transform. speedup Round expected observed values reduce duplicate points, following ggpop's internal fastqq-style layout. base_size Base font size tidyplot-style ggpop theme. base_family Base font family tidyplot-style ggpop theme. lambda_size Font size genomic inflation factor annotation. show.legend Legend display. inherit.aes Inherit plot aesthetics.

title: Population genomics statistics plots — geom_stats
text: Plot windowed population genomics summary statistics along chromosome, selected region, multiple chromosomes. default, selected statistics stacked vertically aligned x-axis, following pixy plotting style.

title: Population genomics statistics plots — geom_stats
code: geom_stats(   mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data$pos/1e+06, y = .data$value),   data = NULL,   ...,   stat = "all",   chr = NULL,   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   geom = c("line", "point"),   colour_by = c("stat", "chr"),   size = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   base_size = 11,   base_family = "",   palette = "publication",   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )  plot_stats(   data,   stat = "all",   chr = NULL,   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   geom = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   base_size = 11,   base_family = "",   palette = "publication",   ... )

title: Population genomics statistics plots — geom_stats
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Defaults window midpoint Mb statistic value. data ggpop_stats object import_stats(). ... Additional geom parameters. stat Statistic names plot, e.g. c("fst", "pi") "". chr Optional chromosome vector. start, end Optional genomic region boundaries base pairs. geom Draw "line" "point" layers. plot_stats() chooses line one chromosome point multiple chromosomes. colour_by Colour statistic chromosome. size, alpha Layer appearance. Defaults follow pixy plotting examples. base_size, base_family Base theme font controls. palette ggpop discrete palette. na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes Standard ggplot2 layer arguments. title, subtitle, caption Optional plot labels. default title added.

title: Population genomics statistics plots — geom_stats
text: geom_stats() returns list ggplot layers. plot_stats() returns ggplot object.

title: Population genomics statistics plots — geom_stats
code: pixy_dir <- system.file("extdata", "Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy", package = "ggpop") stats <- import_stats(pixy_dir, type = "pixy") stats |> plot_stats(stat = c("fst", "pi"), chr = "chr2L")  stats |> plot_stats(chr = "chr2L", start = 1, end = 20000)  stats |> ggpop() + geom_stats(stat = "all", chr = c("chr2L", "chr3L"))

what: Create a ggplot object from ggpop data — ggpop
title: Create a ggplot object from ggpop data — ggpop
text: Creates ggplot2 object typed ggpop data users can add ggpop geoms standard `+` syntax.

title: Create a ggplot object from ggpop data — ggpop
code: ggpop(data, mapping = ggplot2::aes(), ..., module = NULL)

title: Create a ggplot object from ggpop data — ggpop
text: data `ggpop_gwas`, `ggpop_pca`, `ggpop_admix` object. mapping Default ggplot2 aesthetic mapping. ... Reserved future module-specific options. module Optional module override.

title: Import admixture proportion files — import_admixture
text: Imports admixture proportions long-format `ggpop_admix` S3 data `plot_admix()` `geom_admix()`. Directories read full multi-K ADMIXTURE result sets. `import_admix()` shorter user-facing alias. `pop_group` supplied, population labels joined `sample_id` drive pophelper-style group facets sorting.

title: Import admixture proportion files — import_admixture
code: import_admixture(file, type = c("auto", "structure", "admixture"), ind = NULL,   pattern = "\\.Q$", recursive = FALSE, pop_group = NULL, ...) import_admix(file, type = c("auto", "structure", "admixture"), ind = NULL,   pattern = "\\.Q$", recursive = FALSE, pop_group = NULL, ...)

title: Import admixture proportion files — import_admixture
text: file Path one ADMIXTURE `.Q` files, directory containing `.Q` files, limited STRUCTURE-like numeric matrix. type Input format: `admixture`, `structure`, `auto`. ind Optional sample label file data frame. pattern File pattern used `file` directory. recursive Search directories recursively. pop_group Optional path data frame two columns, `sample` `pop`, used population group labels, sorting, facets. ... Additional arguments passed `read.table()`.

title: Import GWAS result files — import_gwas
text: Imports supported GWAS results `ggpop_gwas` data frame normalized `chr`, `pos`, `p`, `snp` columns. `improt_gwas()` `prot_gwas()` typo-compatible aliases.

title: Import GWAS result files — import_gwas
code: import_gwas(file, type = c("auto", "gcta", "gemma", "emmax"), ...) improt_gwas(file, type = c("auto", "gcta", "gemma", "emmax"), ...) prot_gwas(file, type = c("auto", "gcta", "gemma", "emmax"), ...)

title: Import GWAS result files — import_gwas
text: file Path GWAS result file. type Input format: `gcta`, `gemma`, `emmax`, `auto`. ... Additional arguments passed `read.table()`.

what: Import or compute PCA results — import_pca
title: Import or compute PCA results — import_pca
text: Imports PCA coordinates `ggpop_pca`, computes PCA using optional `flashpcaR` backend. `pop_group` supplied, PCA plots map population groups ggpop's unified discrete colour scale default.

title: Import or compute PCA results — import_pca
code: import_pca(file, type = c("auto", "plink", "gcta"), eigenval = NULL,   pop_group = NULL, ...) compute_pca(genotype, method = c("flashpca"), pop_group = NULL, ...)

title: Import or compute PCA results — import_pca
text: file Path PLINK GCTA eigenvector file. type Input format: `plink`, `gcta`, `auto`. eigenval Optional eigenvalue file. pop_group Optional path data frame two columns, `sample` `pop`, used population colors. genotype Genotype matrix flashpcaR-supported input. method PCA method. Currently `flashpca`, backed `flashpcaR`. ... Additional arguments.

title: Import population group labels — import_pop_group
text: Imports two-column population group file PCA colours admixture group labels. default format `sample pop`.

title: Import population group labels — import_pop_group
code: import_pop_group(file, sample_col = "sample", pop_col = "pop", ...)

title: Import population group labels — import_pop_group
text: file Path delimited text file. sample_col Sample ID column name. Defaults `sample`. pop_col Population group column name. Defaults `pop`. ... Additional arguments passed `read.table()`.

title: Import population group labels — import_pop_group
code: groups <- import_pop_group(system.file("extdata", "pop_group.txt", package = "ggpop"))

what: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
title: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
text: Import windowed population genomics statistics FST, pi, Dxy, Watterson's theta, Tajima's D tidy ggpop plotting.

title: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
code: import_stats(dir = NULL, ..., type = c("pixy", "vcftools", "auto"))

title: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
text: dir Directory containing pixy vcftools result files. Files auto-discovered suffix. ... Optional named files pi = "pixy_pi.txt" fst = "pixy_fst.txt". Relative paths resolved inside dir. type Input format. Supports "pixy", "vcftools", "auto".

title: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
text: ggpop_stats data frame normalized columns: stat, chr, start, end, pos, value.

title: Import population genomics summary statistics — import_stats
code: pixy_dir <- system.file("extdata", "Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy", package = "ggpop") stats <- import_stats(pixy_dir, type = "pixy") stats |> plot_stats(stat = c("fst", "pi"), chr = "chr2L")

title: Publication-oriented admixture plots — plot_admix
text: `plot_admix()` ggpop's native ggplot wrapper always returns ggplot object. accepts full multi-K `ggpop_admix` data `import_admix()` filters `k`; `k = ""` renders joined K panels supported pophelper `plotQ(imgoutput = "join")` style. `pop_group`, supports individual labels, sorted individuals, group labels, group-aware sorting. `plot_admixture_pophelper()` converts ggpop long-form admixture data pophelper qlist calls `pophelper::plotQ()` direct original-package compatibility; return structure follows pophelper.

title: Publication-oriented admixture plots — plot_admix
code: plot_admix(data, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL,   sort = c("none", "cluster", "all", "label"), sortind = NULL,   k = "all", palette = NULL, group = "pop", order_group = FALSE,   show_group_labels = NULL, subset_group = NULL, show_legend = FALSE,   show_sample_labels = FALSE, base_size = 5, base_family = "",   legend_position = "top", bar_width = 1, ...) as_pophelper_qlist(data) plot_admixture_pophelper(data, ..., exportplot = FALSE, returnplot = TRUE,   theme = "theme_bw", basesize = 8)

title: Publication-oriented admixture plots — plot_admix
text: data `ggpop_admix` object. title, subtitle, caption Plot text. sort, sortind Native pophelper-compatible sample ordering modes. k K selection: `""`, single integer, integer vector `c(2, 4)`. palette Named unnamed cluster colours. group, order_group, show_group_labels, subset_group Population group column pophelper-style group controls. show_legend, show_sample_labels Legend sample-label display switches. base_size, base_family, legend_position Admixture plot theme settings aligned supported pophelper `plotQ()` subset. bar_width Admixture bar width. ... Additional arguments passed `geom_admix()` `pophelper::plotQ()`. exportplot, returnplot, theme, basesize Arguments forwarded `pophelper::plotQ()`.

title: Publication-oriented admixture plots — plot_admix
text: recommended user-facing admixture plotting interfaces `plot_admix()` `geom_admix()`. `plot_admixture_pophelper()` advanced compatibility escape hatch users need original pophelper behavior.

title: Publication-oriented Manhattan plots — plot_manha
text: High-level publication-oriented Manhattan plot builder. `plot_manha()` tidy extension path `ggpop(data) + geom_manha()` share ggpop's internal fastman-style ggplot implementation.

title: Publication-oriented Manhattan plots — plot_manha
code: plot_manha(data, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL,   threshold = 5e-8, suggestive = 1e-5,   threshold_color = .gwas_threshold_color(),   suggestive_color = .gwas_suggestive_color(), point_size = 0.9,   point_alpha = NA, base_size = 11, base_family = "", legend_position = "none",   logp = TRUE, maxP = 14, bybp = FALSE, palette = "manhattan",   binary = FALSE, ...)

title: Publication-oriented Manhattan plots — plot_manha
text: data `ggpop_gwas` object. title, subtitle, caption Plot text. threshold, suggestive Manhattan reference p-value thresholds. threshold_color, suggestive_color Reference-line colours. Defaults use unified ggpop publication palette can overridden explicitly. point_size, point_alpha Point appearance. base_size, base_family, legend_position Publication theme settings. logp, maxP, bybp Core fastman-style controls forwarded `plot_manha()` mirrored `geom_manha()`. palette ggpop palette name character vector hex colours Manhattan plots. binary `TRUE`, repeat two palette colours across chromosomes binary alternating Manhattan plot. `FALSE`, use one discrete colour per chromosome. ... Additional layer backend arguments.

title: Publication-oriented PCA plots — plot_pca
text: High-level publication-oriented PCA plot builder. `pop_group` supplied `import_pca()`, population groups mapped unified ggpop discrete colour scale default.

title: Publication-oriented PCA plots — plot_pca
code: plot_pca(data, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL,   pc_x = 1, pc_y = 2, point_size = 1.8, point_alpha = 0.85,   base_size = 11, base_family = "", legend_position = "right",   palette = NULL, ...)

title: Publication-oriented PCA plots — plot_pca
text: data `ggpop_pca` object. title, subtitle, caption Plot text. pc_x, pc_y Principal components plot. point_size, point_alpha Point appearance. base_size, base_family, legend_position Publication theme settings. palette Discrete ggpop palette name hex vector population colours. ... Additional layer arguments.

title: Publication-oriented GWAS Q-Q plots — plot_qq
text: High-level publication-oriented Q-Q plot builder. direct path `import_gwas(...) |> plot_qq()`, tidy extension path `import_gwas(...) |> ggpop() + geom_qq()`.

title: Publication-oriented GWAS Q-Q plots — plot_qq
code: plot_qq(data, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL,   show_lambda = TRUE, point_size = 0.8, point_alpha = 0.8,   base_size = 11, base_family = "", legend_position = "none", ...)

title: Publication-oriented GWAS Q-Q plots — plot_qq
text: data `ggpop_gwas` object. title, subtitle, caption Plot text. show_lambda Display genomic inflation factor Q-Q plots. point_size, point_alpha Point appearance. base_size, base_family, legend_position Publication theme settings. ... Additional layer backend arguments.

title: pophelper compatibility layer — pophelper_compat
text: Advanced compatibility wrappers full exported `pophelper` API. Regular users prefer `import_admix(...) |> plot_admix(k = ...)` `import_admix(...) |> ggpop() + geom_admix(k = ...)`. helpers directly call original `pophelper` functions full feature parity required.

title: pophelper compatibility layer — pophelper_compat
code: pophelper_functions() pophelper_call(.fn, ...) import_pophelper_qlist(qlist, source = "pophelper") pophelper_as_qlist(qlist, ...) pophelper_is_qlist(qlist) read_pophelper_q(files = NULL, filetype = "auto", indlabfromfile = FALSE,   readci = FALSE, as_ggpop = FALSE) plot_pophelper_q(data, ..., exportplot = FALSE, returnplot = TRUE,   theme = "theme_bw", basesize = 8) plot_pophelper_q_multiline(data, ..., exportplot = FALSE, returnplot = TRUE,   theme = "theme_bw", basesize = 8) align_pophelper_k(data, ...) sort_pophelper_q(data, ...) split_pophelper_q(data, ...) merge_pophelper_q(data, ...) join_pophelper_q(data, ...) tabulate_pophelper_q(data, ...) summarise_pophelper_q(data, ...) analyse_pophelper_q(data, ...) evanno_pophelper_structure(data, ...) pophelper_distruct_colours(...) pophelper_verify_grplab(grplab)

title: pophelper compatibility layer — pophelper_compat
text: .fn pophelper exported function name. ... Arguments forwarded matching `pophelper` function. qlist pophelper qlist qlist-like list. source Source label converted ggpop admixture data. files, filetype, indlabfromfile, readci Arguments forwarded `pophelper::readQ()`. as_ggpop Convert `readQ()` output `ggpop_admix`. data `ggpop_admix` object, pophelper qlist, tabulated pophelper data frame, STRUCTURE summary data depending wrapped function. exportplot, returnplot, theme, basesize Plotting arguments forwarded pophelper plotting functions. grplab Group label data frame passed `pophelper::verifyGrplab()`.

title: pophelper compatibility layer — pophelper_compat
text: `pophelper_functions()` returns covered pophelper export list. `pophelper_call()` generic escape hatch listed original function. High-level wrappers convert `ggpop_admix` long-form data pophelper qlists original function's expected input.

title: Discrete ggpop population palettes — pop_palettes
text: Unified discrete-palette API population-genomics categorical variables. Palettes follow tidyplots-style behavior: extra colours can downsampled, short palettes interpolated categories requested.

title: Discrete ggpop population palettes — pop_palettes
code: new_pop_palette(x, name = "ggpop palette", reverse = FALSE) ggpop_palette(n = NULL, palette = c("population", "admixture", "manhattan", "publication"),   downsample = c("evenly", "first", "last", "middle"), reverse = FALSE,   saturation = 1) scale_colour_ggpop(palette = c("population", "admixture", "manhattan", "publication"),   n = NULL, saturation = 1, reverse = FALSE,   downsample = c("evenly", "first", "last", "middle"), ...) scale_color_ggpop(palette = c("population", "admixture", "manhattan", "publication"),   n = NULL, saturation = 1, reverse = FALSE,   downsample = c("evenly", "first", "last", "middle"), ...) scale_fill_ggpop(palette = c("admixture", "population", "publication", "manhattan"),   n = NULL, saturation = 1, reverse = FALSE,   downsample = c("evenly", "first", "last", "middle"), ...)

title: Discrete ggpop population palettes — pop_palettes
code: ggpop_palette(4, "population") #> [1] "#4E79A7" "#59A14F" "#76B7B2" "#EDC948" ggplot2::ggplot(data.frame(pop = c("A", "B"), x = 1:2, y = 1:2),   ggplot2::aes(x, y, colour = pop)) +   ggplot2::geom_point() +   scale_colour_ggpop("population")

title: ggpop theme helpers — theme_tidyplot
text: Theme helpers ggpop plots. `theme_tidyplot()` primary tidyplot-style theme used PCA GWAS modules. minimal variants selectively keep one axes visible.

title: ggpop theme helpers — theme_tidyplot
code: theme_tidyplot(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "") theme_ggplot2(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "") theme_minimal_xy(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "") theme_minimal_x(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "") theme_minimal_y(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "") style_void(plot, fontsize = 7, base_family = "")

title: ggpop theme helpers — theme_tidyplot
text: plot ggplot object. fontsize Base font size. base_family Base font family.

title: Publication-oriented ggpop styles — theme_ggpop_publication
text: Helpers publication-oriented ggpop figures: clean theme, colour palettes, discrete fill/colour scales.

title: Publication-oriented ggpop styles — theme_ggpop_publication
code: theme_ggpop_publication(base_size = 11, base_family = "",   legend_position = "top", grid = c("major", "none", "both")) ggpop_palette(n, palette = c("publication", "admixture", "manhattan")) scale_fill_ggpop(n = NULL, palette = c("admixture", "publication"), ...) scale_colour_ggpop(n = NULL, palette = c("manhattan", "publication"), ...)

title: Publication-oriented ggpop styles — theme_ggpop_publication
text: base_size, base_family Base text size font family passed ggplot2 themes. legend_position Legend placement accepted ggplot2. grid Panel grid style. n Number colours. palette Palette family. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2 scale constructor.
